transformation_type: anti dihydroxylation of alkenes via halonium opening and carboxylate displacement
mechanism_type: iodonium-ion formation followed by double carboxylate substitution and hydrolysis
reactant_pattern_text: alkene + iodine + silver carboxylate under anhydrous or partially hydrated conditions
product_pattern_text: trans-1,2-diol or cis-1,2-diol depending on Prévost versus Woodward-Brutcher conditions
common_conditions: dry conditions favor trans diols while added water or acetic acid in the Woodward-Brutcher modification favors cis-diols
description_short: Classical vicinal dihydroxylation manifold that uses iodine and silver carboxylates to convert alkenes into stereodefined 1,2-diols under either dry or wet conditions.

mechanism_type: concerted syn oxygen transfer from a peroxyacid to an alkene
common_conditions: usually run at low to ambient temperature in nonprotic solvent with buffering additives when acid-sensitive products are present
description_short: Foundational alkene epoxidation method in which a peroxyacid delivers oxygen in a stereospecific syn process to give epoxides from isolated double bonds.

transformation_type: acid-catalyzed coupling of alkenes with aldehydes to form oxocarbenium-derived oxygen heterocycles and alcohols
mechanism_type: protonated aldehyde addition to alkene followed by carbocation capture or elimination
reactant_pattern_text: alkene + aldehyde under protic or Lewis acid conditions
common_conditions: acid activates the aldehyde to an oxocarbenium precursor and product class depends on nucleophile availability and ring-closing opportunity
description_short: Versatile acid-promoted alkene–aldehyde coupling that channels oxocarbenium chemistry into tetrahydropyrans, 1,3-dioxanes, allylic alcohols, and related oxygenated products.

transformation_type: tandem Prins cyclization and pinacol rearrangement to build tetrahydrofuran frameworks
mechanism_type: oxocarbenium formation followed by cyclization and carbocationic [1,2]-shift
reactant_pattern_text: allylic 1,2-diol or allylic acetal + aldehyde or ketone under Lewis acid activation
product_pattern_text: highly substituted tetrahydrofuran, annulated 3-acyl tetrahydrofuran, or cyclopentanone-annulated product
common_conditions: typically low-temperature Lewis-acid activation of an acetal or allylic diol followed by tandem cyclization and rearrangement
description_short: Powerful tandem cationic cascade that merges Prins cyclization with pinacol rearrangement to forge densely substituted tetrahydrofuran and annulated ring systems.

transformation_type: activation of sulfoxides to α-functionalized sulfides or carbonyl-equivalent products
mechanism_type: acylsulfonium formation, sulfur-stabilized cation generation, and nucleophile capture
reactant_pattern_text: sulfoxide activated by acylating or silylating reagent in the presence of nucleophile
product_pattern_text: α-substituted sulfide, vinyl sulfide, aldehyde, or carbonyl/thiol pair after hydrolysis
common_conditions: often uses acetic anhydride, trifluoroacetic anhydride, or silyl triflates with optional acid/base co-catalysis to generate sulfur-substituted cationic intermediates
description_short: General sulfoxide activation strategy that converts sulfoxides into electrophilic sulfur-stabilized intermediates and enables α-functionalization, rearrangement, and carbonyl generation.
